title: After Work Launch
objective: Survive a 2-4 minute after-hours rooftop escape while weaving through escalating office hazards, grabbing clock-out stamps to build a score multiplier, and avoiding three hits that end the run.

controls.outcome: Move between rooftop lanes to dodge incoming hazards and line up score pickups.
Hop over low hazards like cables, carts, and spilled coffee streams.
Slip under high hazards like swinging signs, drones, and flying folders.

steps: The player starts on a dusk skyline catwalk outside a glowing office tower, trying to make it home after work.
Hazards stream in waves: paperwork gusts, rolling office chairs, dangling cables, delivery drones, and snapping neon signs.
Every few seconds a 'clock-out stamp' spawns in the most dangerous lane; collecting it refreshes and increases the score multiplier.
A siren cue announces a 'launch shift' twist: all hazard lanes rotate one step left or right, forcing a fast route change.
Wave speed rises over time, and mixed-height hazards begin stacking so the player must combine lane shifts with hops and slides.
The run ends after three hits or one direct collision with a red-alert hazard during the final overtime rush.
scoring: Score increases from survival time and near-miss dodges. Each collected clock-out stamp raises the multiplier by 1x, but missing a stamp resets the multiplier to 1x. Consecutive near-misses without taking damage add a
tips: Play for near-misses early; the base waves are slow enough to farm combo safely.
Do not chase every clock-out stamp if a launch shift siren just sounded.
Use burst step for low clutter only; lane movement is safer when hazards are stacked.
Audio matters: stamp chimes signal multiplier windows, and the siren warns the lane rotation twist before it happens.

trendContext.title: After 3 years of work, my indie tower defense Bean Beasts just launched today on Switch! 🫘
